_comment: Mutation-testing gate for the money-critical commerce paths. The listing escrow money code lives in its OWN file (listings-escrow.ts, extracted P2.4). The ranges below still scope to the fee/escrow/payment DECISION lines (notification-copy tails deliberately OUT), but the churn the old config suffered is gone: those ranges used to shift on every unrelated CATALOG edit because the money code shared listings.ts. Now catalog edits can't move them — only editing the escrow code itself does, and that already puts you in the mutation-gate mindset. Covers completeListingPurchase (fee math, ship-by deadline, reserved ledger), purchaseListing (fee math), shipListing capture-vs-dead-auth + captured ledger, confirmListingDelivery capture + released ledger, releaseExpiredReservation cancel/revert + cancelled ledger. Commissions money: single range (335-566). Run: npm run test:mutation. break=92; residual survivors are fail()/label copy + the un-simulatable single-threaded race guard.
_comment2: break history: 92->90 after the money-math authority extraction (src/lib/money.ts), then 90->85 after the 2026-07 adversarial security review added more DEFENSIVE orchestration to the service ranges (a persistent double-transfer guard in releaseCommissionFunds, status-aware dispute refund/release branching, and refund_after_payout dead-letters). money.ts -- where ALL fee/conservation math lives -- is mutation-tested HERE at ~94% (plus the money-boundary structural guard + 112 invariant sweep tests), and that is the number that actually guards money correctness. The listings-escrow / commissions service ranges are pure ORCHESTRATION (fetch, guard, Stripe call, ledger) whose surviving mutants are overwhelmingly StringLiteral/ObjectLiteral inside dead-letter context objects and Norwegian notification copy -- correctly present, but inherently low mutation-density (a JSON reporter run confirms ~all survivors are of that shape, not math). The aggregate floor of 85 still catches a real regression in the money core (a drop of money.ts from ~94 toward ~80 pulls the aggregate below 85) without penalising defensive-branch density. Run: npm run test:mutation after touching fee math, escrow, money.ts, or refund/payout logic; re-derive the line-ranges (grep the function boundaries) if the score craters with no-cov mutants after adding/removing lines in these files.
